reasoning: An invasion of Iran by the US to establish territorial control requires a monumental shift in US foreign policy, which is highly unlikely to materialize within the short late-2025 timeframe. Even under a highly belligerent administration or following a major Iranian provocation, stand-off airstrikes or naval actions are far more likely than a ground invasion intended to hold territory. The base rate for such an event is near zero. The specified timeframe is also limited (only ~6.5 months in late 2025). The community/market currently assigns about 3.35% probability. Given extreme tail risks, a ~2.5% estimate respects the market signal while heavily anchoring to the overwhelming historical and logistical base rate against a US ground invasion of Iran.
